recommendations: add a minimal CI smoke test for the MCP plugin (`python mcp_output/start_mcp.py --help` + import checks)
pin and validate runtime dependencies across both project and plugin (single source of truth for `nltk`
`fastmcp`
`pydantic`
plus version matrix)
add contract tests for every exposed MCP endpoint (especially class constructors vs callable functions) with golden input/output fixtures
introduce robust error mapping in the adapter layer (NLTK corpus-missing
network failures in `Translator`
invalid training data) to consistent MCP error responses
create a startup preflight in the plugin that checks required NLTK corpora and returns actionable install commands
harden packaging metadata consistency (analysis detected `pyproject` while tree shows legacy files—standardize on one build config and verify wheel/sdist builds)
add offline-safe test markers and mocks for translation/network-dependent paths to eliminate flaky tests
enforce static quality gates (ruff/flake8 + mypy or pyright on plugin boundary types) and pre-commit hooks
reduce API surface risk by curating/whitelisting high-value endpoints instead of exposing every class symbol directly
add observability hooks (structured logs
per-endpoint latency/error counters) to monitor MCP reliability and performance
include security guardrails for exposed endpoints (input size limits
timeout/circuit breaker for long NLP ops
safe deserialization checks for format loaders)
add user-focused MCP docs with concrete recipes (tokenization
sentiment
classification training) and failure troubleshooting
create regression benchmarks for heavy paths (`_text.py`
classifier training/inference) and track them in CI
add compatibility tests across Python versions and common NLTK/pattern availability scenarios
add integration tests for `python -m textblob.download_corpora` flows (lite/full) and verify plugin behavior before/after corpus install

success_factors: End-to-end workflow status is success, with all planned nodes executed (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-based adapter strategy was appropriate for TextBlob (high import feasibility 0.9 / 0.93 confidence).
MCP plugin started healthy over stdio and passed plugin-level validation.
Low intrusiveness risk reduced integration friction with the upstream codebase.
failure_reasons: Original project tests were not validated in this run despite being marked with 100% coverage metadata, creating a verification gap.
DeepWiki analysis failed, reducing external architectural/context enrichment.
Node-level timing and resource telemetry were not captured, limiting diagnostics precision.

node_performance.generation_time: Plugin generation completed with expected file set and import adapter mode; no per-node timing available.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin health test passed, but original project test execution appears absent/insufficiently reported.
